summary: Implemented and verified review queue/semantic job settlement progress. The app now supports review:settle dry-run/apply semantics, writes review_queue_settlement_actions, auto-holds generated behavior/legacy generated-memory candidates without promoting them, excludes held records from default retrieval, and includes the action report in status freshness. Applied the safe auto-hold settlement to the current data vault: review queue open backlog dropped from 588 to 73, 515 items are closed/settled, semantic jobs now have 515 settled and 73 manual review holds, and monitoring freshness is healthy at 11/11. App commit 931f74c was pushed to origin/main. Data repo remains intentionally unsynced because existing dirty/untracked candidate/review/raw-adjacent state is broad and needs scoped sync policy.
decisions: Treat review:settle as an actual settlement gate instead of only a classification report.
Do not auto-approve generated memories; only auto-hold deterministic generated behavior/legacy generated-memory backlog and keep manual semantic review visible.
Add review_queue_settlement_actions as a required status freshness artifact so applied settlement proof cannot go stale invisibly.
Keep data repo sync separate because the current data tree includes broad dirty/untracked candidate/review/session-derived artifacts outside a safe scoped push.
next_steps: Resolve the remaining 73 manual semantic review candidates with a scoped reviewer workflow: approve, reject, merge, or hold with evidence.
Repair the remaining finish-gate lifecycle blockers: 5 manual stale reviews and 1 trace reconstruction.
Implement real dense vector retrieval and explicit rag-golden.json so RAG eval can move beyond lexical_fallback_v2.
Design a data sync policy for committing review settlement state without pushing raw/session-derived local-only records.
